prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response:
You’re probably wrong about how much others are judging you.

Most people spend far more time thinking about themselves—how they appear, what they said, what they need—than scrutinizing you. That social slip-up, awkward comment, or choice of clothing you can’t stop replaying in your mind likely went unnoticed or was forgotten moments later by others.

We tend to overestimate our visibility and the critical attention directed our way. Psychologists call this the *spotlight effect*. So, unless you’ve genuinely hurt someone, chances are the critical spotlight you feel is mostly in your own head.
